title: modRNAS THAT EXPRESS ANTI MULLERIAN HORMONE (AMH) AND USES THEREOF
abstract: The present disclosure is directed to modRNAs encoding the anti-Mullerian hormone (AMH) methods for improving follicular health and natural conception using these modRNAs. The disclosure is also directed to methods for protecting the follicular reserve from gonadotoxic attacks from chemotherapy or radiotherapy using modRNAs encoding AMH.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method, comprising subjecting an ovarian follicle cell to a modified RNA (modRNA) encoding anti- Mullerian hormone (AMH), wherein the modRNA comprises pseudouridine nucleotides and does not comprise uridine nucleotides. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the modRNA further comprises a 5' untranslated region (UTR). 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the modRNA further comprises a 3' UTR. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the modRNA is codon optimized. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the modRNA is modified to have an increased G/C content as compared to a native RNA encoding AMH. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the modRNA comprises a sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 2. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the ovarian follicle cell is a granulosa cell (GC). 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the cell is contacted by the modRNA in vitro. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the cell is contacted by the modRNA in vivo in a subject. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the contacting is achieved by injection or by topical application of the modRNA to the subject. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the subject is undergoing, will be undergoing or has undergone chemotherapy. 12. The method of claim 9 or claim 11, further comprising performing conventional hormonal hyperstimulation and in vitro fertilization (IVF) on the subject. 13. A modified RNA (modRNA) encoding anti-Mullerian hormone (AMH), wherein the modRNA comprises pseudouridine nucleotides and does not comprise uridine nucleotides. 14. The modRNA of claim 1, further comprising a 5' untranslated region (UTR). 15. The modRNA of claim 14, further comprising a 3' UTR. 16. The modRNA of any one of claims 13-15, wherein the modRNA is codon optimized. 17. The modRNA of any one of claims 13-16, wherein the modRNA is modified to have an increased G/C content as compared to a native RNA encoding AMH. 18. The modRNA of claim 13, wherein the modRNA comprises a sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 2. 19. A method for improving resistance of an ovarian follicle cell to gonadotoxicity in a subject, comprising administering a modified RNA (modRNA) encoding anti-Mullerian hormone (AMH) to the subject, wherein the modRNA comprises pseudouridine nucleotides and does not comprise uridine nucleotides. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the modRNA further comprises a 5' untranslated region (UTR). 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the modRNA further comprises a 3' UTR. 22. The method of any one of claims 19-21, wherein the modRNA is codon optimized. 23. The method of any one of claims 19-22, wherein the modRNA is modified to have an increased G/C content as compared to a native RNA encoding AMH. 24. The method of claim 19, wherein the modRNA comprises a sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 2. 25. The method of any one of claims 19-24, wherein the administering is achieved by injecting the modRNA to the ovaries of the subject. 26. The method of any one of claims 19-24, wherein the administering is achieved by topical application of the modRNA to the subject. 27. The method of any one of claims 19-26, wherein the ovarian follicle cell is a granulosa cell (GC). 28. The method of any one of claims 19-27, wherein the modRNA is expressed in the ovarian follicle cell. 29. The method of any one of claims 19-28, wherein the gonadotoxicity is caused by chemotherapy. 30. The method of any one of claims 19-28, wherein the gonadotoxicity is caused by radiotherapy.
